setting.note: Brussels, thinly disguised as Villette in the kingdom of Labassecour. Brontë mapped her own two years teaching at the Pensionnat Heger on the Rue d'Isabelle into it: the boarding school, the old Flemish streets, the park across the way, the cathedral where she once made confession. It brings Belgium into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Charlotte Brontë, first published 1853; the author died in 1855.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1855).
rights.attribution: Charlotte Brontë, Villette (1853). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The stained-glass violet of the old church where Lucy makes her confession, its gloom 'not gilded but purpled by light'. Taken straight from the text; clears WCAG AA on paper.
